steps.title: Apply the margin of error
steps.content:
The estimate is $14\%$, and the margin of error is $2.75\%$. Subtract and add the margin to get the plausible interval.
$$14\%-2.75\%=11.25\%$$
$$14\%+2.75\%=16.75\%$$

steps.title: Interpret the interval
steps.content: The conclusion should describe the percent of all gloves produced during the week as plausibly between $11.25\%$ and $16.75\%$, not exactly equal to the sample estimate. The answer is B.
